commit.message:
fix: restrict High Odds to Kalshi/Polymarket; fix close_time datetime type

- find_near_certainty_markets: skip sportsbook platforms (DraftKings,
  FanDuel, BetMGM, Caesars, Pinnacle) — their decimal odds prices are
  not comparable to a 0-1 probability price
- NearCertaintyMarket.close_time: accept datetime | str | None so Pydantic
  does not raise a ValidationError when contracts carry a datetime object
  instead of a pre-serialised ISO string (was crashing the scan cycle)

commit.message:
fix: realize ENGINE_HANDLER expands convention-format attributes and relationships

The inference engine outputs YAML in convention format (attributes as "Type modifiers"
strings, relationships as object maps). The realize engine's template generators expect
expanded format (attributes as [{name, type, required, ...}] arrays).

The ENGINE_HANDLER now converts between formats:
- String attributes → {name, type, required, unique, auto}
- Object relationships → [{name, type, target, ...}] arrays
- Entity names injected from YAML keys

This fixes:
- ORM schema generation (Prisma schema now generates correctly)
- undefined controller/route names
- All template generators receive properly formatted data

Result: 465 files generated from guesthouse spec via self-hosted CLI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Secret interception in pre-prompt hook — paste key, it's stored, Claude never sees it

User types: "onboard fmp abc123def456"
Hook BLOCKS the prompt → extracts key → age encrypts → shreds temp
Claude never processes the prompt. Key never enters context.

4 detection patterns:
  1. "onboard SERVICE KEY" → explicit onboarding
  2. "SERVICE key: KEY" → labeled paste
  3. Raw key paste (sk-*, ghp_*, sk-ant-*, pplx-*) → auto-detect service
  4. "key: VALUE" → pending (asks which service next)

Fallback: if no age encryption, stores in .env with warning.

This is the TERMINAL-ONLY solution. No browser, no separate terminal,
no ! prefix. User pastes in normal prompt, hook intercepts.

v2.2.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

cron_poller._execute_job():

- 直接 POST，不繞中間層

- 只做一件事：建卡片

fix: align EC2, EBS, and S3 generators to official Elastic field schemas

- EC2: replace aws.ec2.metrics.* CloudWatch block with official flat fields
  (cpu.total.pct, network.in/out.*, diskio.read/write.*, status.check_failed*)
- EBS: fix VolumeReadOps/WriteOps/ReadBytes/WriteBytes and VolumeConsumedReadWriteOps
  aggregation type from .sum to .avg per metricbeat exported-fields spec
- S3 s3_request: add missing requests.delete/head/post/select/list fields,
  select_scanned/returned bytes, latency.first_byte.ms; remove non-standard
  bytes_per_period fields

Fix mailto: not opening - don't encode email addresses in To field

The @ symbol was being encoded by encodeURIComponent which broke
the mailto: URL. Email addresses in the To field must remain unencoded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Link credit sales between attendant handover & accounts module

Replace free-text credit sales input with itemized line-item entry
that creates real CreditSale records in the accounts module. Attendants
now select account, fuel type, and volume — the system auto-computes
the amount using the account's custom rate or global fuel price.

Backend: new GET /credit-accounts endpoint, credit item processing in
submit_handover with deduplication, over-limit handling, and pre-existing
shift credit sale detection. Frontend: line-item table in my-shift Step 3,
credit sale details in handover review, custom rate display on account cards.
All new fields are optional — fully backward compatible.

commit.message:
Poseidon CLI Wrapper v2.2.0 — LLM-agnostic platform launcher

4 new files (1,082 lines):

banner.ts (326 lines):
  Gold trident ASCII logo with gradient (gold→bronze)
  System stats: skills, rules, learning score, errors, project, secrets
  Context progress bar: green→yellow→orange→red gradient (20 chars)
  Percentage number color matches bar leading edge
  Responsive to terminal width

llm-registry.ts (126 lines):
  LLM abstraction: claude, gemini, codex, ollama
  One --llm flag swaps the entire backend
  Each LLM has: command, args, version detection, capabilities

poseidon.ts (475 lines):
  Main CLI: banner → project picker → IPC server → spawn LLM → post-exit
  Commands: onboard, projects, status, update, version, help
  IPC server on Unix socket for secure secret collection (stty -echo)
  Lifecycle: pre-launch validation, post-exit cleanup

poseidon-ipc.ts (155 lines):
  IPC client called from inside LLM session (Bash tool)
  Connects to wrapper's Unix socket for secret collection
  Stores via SecretClient after receiving from wrapper

Usage: poseidon [--llm claude|gemini|codex|ollama] [--project NAME] [-r]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(mint-docs): add LLM quick reference page to prevent hallucination (#1842)

* docs(mint-docs): add llms.txt with SDK/data-fetching summary #1841

Add a comprehensive llms.txt file to prevent LLM hallucination when
generating Vertz UI code. The "Critical Patterns" section documents
the correct data-fetching pattern (SDK method calls to query(), not
raw entity name strings), along with forms, reactivity, styling,
entity, and schema patterns.

Closes #1841

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Codegen runs automatically during `vertz dev` and builds — no need
to mention `vertz codegen` as a manual step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(mint-docs): fix .hidden() → .is('hidden') in llms.txt #1841

The column builder has no .hidden() method — the correct API is
.is('hidden'). Also documents .is('sensitive') annotation.

See #1844 for the same issue in other docs pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(mint-docs): replace custom llms.txt with auto-generated + quick reference page #1841

Delete the hand-maintained llms.txt and let Mintlify auto-generate
it from the navigation structure. Move the critical patterns content
(data fetching, forms, reactivity, entities, schema) into a dedicated
`guides/llm-quick-reference.mdx` page so LLMs see it in the
auto-generated index.

feat: fortune-teller uses MppCharge extractor + Rust client with send_with_payment

  MppCharge<FortunePrice> handles 402/verify automatically.
  Session/stream endpoints remain manual.

  402 → pay → retry flow with ZcashPaymentProvider.

Tested end-to-end on testnet with real shielded payment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix terrain scoring: all types now compete equally via noise regions

Previously grass/loam had affinity bases of 0.5+ while others started at
0.1, making them always win regardless of slider weights. Rocky terrain
only appeared on high elevation, chalky only with high aridity, etc.

New scoring: each type's score = weight * (noise_field + small_env_bias).
The noise field (0.0-1.0) dominates and creates coherent spatial regions.
Environmental bias is capped at ±0.15 for subtle natural variation.
All types use the same noise frequency (0.06) for similar region sizes.

Result: setting chalky to 50% actually produces 50% chalky terrain,
not 2% because the environmental conditions weren't met.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update project memory — MD shows all peptides drift, honest assessment

6-mers drift on full ERAP2 (real result, not fragment artifact).
11-mer drifted on cropped channel (invalid, fragment denatured).
11-mer on full ERAP2 running now — last computational test.
V2+V4 hybrid design (protein anchor + peptide tail) in progress.
Wet lab IC50 (~$1,300) is the definitive next step regardless.

Key lesson: Boltz-2 ipTM != binding affinity.

commit.message:
feat: generate-flow triple mode — Assembler, Plugin API, or HTML capture (1.9.5)

Adds Plugin API (use_figma) as a third output mode for generate-flow, matching
the dual-mode pattern from create-component. All three modes now documented
with a comparison table showing tradeoffs.

- Option A (Assembler): real FM Kit instances, Figma variable bindings, editable
- Option B (Plugin API): direct JS build, faster, hex with token comments
- Option C (HTML capture): flat vectors from HTML, last resort

Includes FM token-to-hex reference table so Plugin API code uses correct values.
Updated cleanup checklist to cover all three modes + generation metadata card.

and didn't add actual protein data. Now it always appends:
- UniProt data (ID, name, gene, organism, function)
- PDB structure data (method, resolution, title)
- BLAST results (identity, coverage, description)

The template provides report structure, but data is appended for AI analysis.

Fix expiry job: expire trial subscriptions (periodMonths=0 with expiresAt)

skipped all periodMonths=0, leaving trials stuck as ACTIVE forever.
Now skip only true lifetime subs (expiresAt=null).

Add tomato disease classes — expand model from 3 to 13 classes

- Added 10 tomato disease classes from PlantVillage dataset
- Notebook: N_CLASSES now derived from dataset, title updated
- API: CLASS_NAMES expanded to 13, treatment info for all classes
- Frontend/Mobile: display names handle both Potato and Tomato prefixes
- Training workflow: downloads and renames tomato classes from Kaggle
- Titles updated to "Plant Disease Detection"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Seed controls entire world: elevation, water table, trees, and terrain

Previously only terrain scoring noise used the seed — elevation and water
table were generated with fixed seeds, making all maps structurally identical.

Now generate_elevation_seeded() and generate_water_table_seeded() incorporate
the seed into their noise hash, so different seeds produce fundamentally
different landscapes: different hills, valleys, wet/dry zones, and tree
distributions.

New regenerate_world_preview() helper regenerates the full chain:
grid → elevation → water table → terrain. Called by Random, Preview,
and Start Game buttons.
